text:
NBCUniversalhas plans to build a larger audience for theParis Olympicswith a parade of high-profile celebrities featured in their 7,000 hours of competi¬≠tion coverage and related programming this summer.

‚ÄúThis kind of celebrity wouldn‚Äôt have been part of NBC Olympics coverage of the past. Now it‚Äôs one of the foundational elements,‚Äù Rick Cordella, president of NBC Sports,said. ‚ÄúWe‚Äôve got to be innovating, trying things differently, trying to match where the media world is in 2024.‚Äù

New year mood. Paris 2024 Olympics. C u this summer ?????pic.twitter.com/vWUXIPdMVZ

This Olympics will break with previous games in having events streamed live on Peacock. Broadcasts of the past aired popular events on tape delay. The change to live streaming will require having different prime-time programming.

The games will start around 3 a.m. and end around 6 p.m. Eastern time. Paris is six hours ahead of Washington, D.C.

NBC plans to use celebrities Kelly Clarkson, Peyton Manning,Jimmy Fallon, andSnoop Doggfor various hosting assignments. Snoop Dogg will provide man-on-the-street interviews, while Clarkson and Manning will host the opening ceremony on July 26. Fallon will host the closing ceremony alongside Mike Tirico, NFL play-by-play announcer on NBC‚ÄôsSunday Night Football.

NBC is aiming for more than 20% of Olympics viewership to come from the Peacock stream¬≠ing platform.

‚ÄúWhether they‚Äôre watching linear or digital, we don‚Äôt really care,‚Äù Dan Lovinger, the NBC ad sales executive,saidtoVariety. ‚ÄúWe mon¬≠etize all platforms. I‚Äôm very confident that this will be the most supported Olympic Games of all time ‚Äî in dollars. That‚Äôs what we count.‚Äù

Beyond the broadcast, the games have been dealing with their own organizational concerns in getting the event prepared in Paris. Recently, a water protection organization cautioned swimmers about thedangerous level of bacteriain a main competitive swimming waterway.

CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM THE WASHINGTON EXAMINER

Organizers also told athletes in February that there will be no air conditioning in the eco-friendly athletic village. The Summer Games are aiming to cut emissions by being the ‚Äúgreenest‚Äù ever competition. Several athletes, such as those on the Australian Olympic Committee, are spending more than $150,000 on air conditioning and fans to deal with the summer heat in Paris.

The Olympic events are scheduled to take place July 25 through Aug. 11.
title: NBC looks to boost Paris Olympics ratings with celebrities including Snoop Dogg and Jimmy Fallon
author: Heather Hunter
description: NBCUniversal has plans to build a larger audience for the Paris Olympics with a parade of high-profile celebrities featured in their 7,000 hours of
